description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, weathered stone blocks, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The structure features a single, wide archway supported by thick, rectangular piers with subtle vertical grooves that evoke the look of pilasters. The top of the arch is flat and solid, with a slightly uneven surface, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The stonework is dark gray, with visible texturing and some minor chipping, adding to its historical character. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, making the design simple and utilitarian. The overall form is reminiscent of Roman or early medieval triumphal arches, emphasizing strength and durability.
description: A weathered stone triumphal arch with a single wide archway, thick piers, and a flat top, featuring a simple, utilitarian design in dark gray stone.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide archway, thick piers, and subtle vertical grooves on the stone blocks.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the arch and the solid, blocky construction of the piers.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the archway and stonework with minor surface irregularities.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the depth and rectangular form of the piers.
